title: Cookbook: Install Input Protection on a New A11y Action (Beginner)
desc: Unified guide: why user-input protection exists, how FocusStealSentinel + ResolveA11yTarget work conceptually, and the step-by-step recipe for adding a new a11y action with full protection. Beginner-friendly copy-paste snippets plus a smoke-test checklist. Supersedes the older sentinel concept skill.
steps: WHY THIS EXISTS. Every a11y action runs on behalf of the user but can accidentally grab the foreground window (SetForegroundWindow, UIA activation on Chromium/Electron, SendInput). If it does, the user loses their typing / dialog / mouse context. Two complementary tools solve this: (1) InputReadiness.Probe shows the magnifier and yields to active user input BEFORE the action fires; (2) FocusStealSentinel watches the foreground during the action and restores + self-reports if theft happened. Find is the only exempt action -- it is legitimate foreground exploration.
author: claude
